nodes.description: Severe, typically non-progressive cerebellar ataxia. Ataxia is often the most debilitating feature; some patients become non-ambulant.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological features of epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Later, non-progressive, cerebellar ataxia and hearing loss were noted.

evidence.reference_title: Neurological features of epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy syndrome.
evidence.snippet: ataxia proved to be the most debilitating feature, with three patients non-ambulant

nodes.description: In the kidney, Kir4.1 sits on the basolateral membrane of the distal convoluted tubule (often as a Kir4.1/Kir5.1 heteromer with KCNJ16), where K+ recycling across the basolateral membrane is required to sustain Na+-K+-ATPase activity and thus normal NaCl reabsorption. Loss of Kir4.1 impairs basolateral K+ recycling, causing renal salt wasting with a Gitelman-like biochemical profile: normotensive hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis, hypomagnesemia, and hypocalciuria. Patient distal tubular cells show reduced basolateral infoldings, reflecting impaired salt-reabsorption capacity.

cell_types: kidney distal convoluted tubule epithelial cell

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: We propose that KCNJ10 is required in the kidney for normal salt reabsorption in the distal convoluted tubule because of the need for K(+) recycling across the basolateral membrane to enable normal activity of the Na(+)-K(+)-ATPase; loss of this function accounts for the observed electrolyte defects.

evidence.reference_title: Epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy, and KCNJ10 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Mice with Kcnj10 deletions became dehydrated, with definitive evidence of renal salt wasting.

evidence.reference_title: KCNJ10 gene mutations causing EAST syndrome (epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, and tubulopathy) disrupt channel function.
evidence.snippet: Kcnj10 and Kcnj16 were found in the basolateral membrane of mouse distal convoluted tubules, connecting tubules, and cortical collecting ducts.

evidence.reference_title: KCNJ10 gene mutations causing EAST syndrome (epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, and tubulopathy) disrupt channel function.
evidence.snippet: EM of distal tubular cells of a patient with EAST syndrome showed reduced basal infoldings in this nephron segment, which likely reflects the morphological consequences of the impaired salt reabsorption capacity.

nodes.description: Hearing aids and supportive audiologic management for the sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: EAST/SeSAME syndrome: Review of the literature and introduction of four new Latvian patients.
evidence.snippet: The treatment is based on antiepileptic drugs, electrolyte replacement, hearing aids and mobility devices.

nodes.description: Hypokalemia with metabolic alkalosis resulting from distal tubule salt wasting, characteristically with normal blood pressure.

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: electrolyte imbalance (hypokalemia, metabolic alkalosis, and hypomagnesemia)

nodes.description: Low serum magnesium due to impaired distal tubule magnesium handling, part of the Gitelman-like biochemical profile.

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: electrolyte imbalance (hypokalemia, metabolic alkalosis, and hypomagnesemia)

nodes.description: In the cochlea, Kir4.1 is expressed in the stria vascularis and is required for the generation of endolymph and the endocochlear potential. Loss of Kir4.1 function impairs endolymph homeostasis and the ionic environment needed for normal hair-cell transduction, producing sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: in cochlea, where it is involved in the generation of endolymph

evidence.reference_title: KCNJ10 mutations disrupt function in patients with EAST syndrome.
evidence.snippet: KCNJ10 is expressed in the kidney distal convoluted tubule, cochlear stria vascularis and brain glial cells.

nodes.description: In the brain and spinal cord, Kir4.1 is expressed in glia, where it takes up extracellular K+ released by neuronal repolarization (spatial potassium buffering) and supports glutamate clearance. Loss of Kir4.1 raises extracellular potassium and impairs glial homeostasis, contributing to the neuronal hyperexcitability that produces seizures and to cerebellar dysfunction causing ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: KCNJ10 is expressed in glia in the brain and spinal cord, where it is believed to take up K(+) released by neuronal repolarization

evidence.reference_title: Epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy, and KCNJ10 mutations.
evidence.snippet: This region contained the KCNJ10 gene, which encodes a potassium channel expressed in the brain, inner ear, and kidney. Sequencing of this candidate gene revealed homozygous missense mutations in affected persons in both families.

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: These findings demonstrate that loss-of-function mutations in KCNJ10 cause this syndrome, which we name SeSAME.

nodes.description: Biallelic missense or nonsense variants in KCNJ10 reduce or abolish the inwardly rectifying potassium current carried by Kir4.1. Heterologous expression of patient mutations in Xenopus oocytes causes significant and specific decreases in potassium currents, and many of the mutations affect highly conserved residues known to cause loss of function in related K+ channels. This is the primary molecular lesion underlying all downstream organ-specific manifestations.
nodes.id: Kir4.1 Potassium Channel Loss of Function

evidence.reference_title: Seizures, sensorineural deafness, ataxia, mental retardation, and electrolyte imbalance (SeSAME syndrome) caused by mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: Direct DNA sequencing of KCNJ10, which encodes an inwardly rectifying K(+) channel, identifies previously unidentified missense or nonsense mutations on both alleles in all affected subjects.

evidence.reference_title: Epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy, and KCNJ10 mutations.
evidence.snippet: These mutations, when expressed heterologously in xenopus oocytes, caused significant and specific decreases in potassium currents.

evidence.reference_title: EAST syndrome: Clinical, pathophysiological, and genetic aspects of mutations in KCNJ10.
evidence.snippet: So far 14 different KCNJ10 mutations have been published which either directly affect channel function or may lead to mislocalisation.

evidence.reference_title: KCNJ10 gene mutations causing EAST syndrome (epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, and tubulopathy) disrupt channel function.
evidence.snippet: When expressed in CHO and HEK293 cells, the KCNJ10 mutations R65P, G77R, and R175Q caused a marked impairment of channel function. R199X showed complete loss of function.

nodes.description: Oral magnesium supplementation to correct hypomagnesemia associated with the distal tubulopathy.

evidence.snippet: The treatment is based on antiepileptic drugs, electrolyte replacement, hearing aids and mobility devices.

nodes.description: A renal salt-losing tubulopathy of the distal convoluted tubule, clinically resembling Gitelman syndrome, with normotensive electrolyte imbalance.

nodes.description: Moderate sensorineural deafness due to impaired Kir4.1-dependent endolymph homeostasis in the cochlea.

evidence.reference_title: Epilepsy, ataxia, sensorineural deafness, tubulopathy, and KCNJ10 mutations.
evidence.snippet: epilepsy beginning in infancy and severe ataxia, moderate sensorineural deafness, and a renal salt-losing tubulopathy with normotensive hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis
